title: Device and method for sensing underwater sound pressure
abstract: A hydrophone may include a first piezoelectric cable including alternating sections of positive polarity and negative polarity, and a second piezoelectric cable including alternating sections of negative polarity and positive polarity. At least a portion of each section of positive polarity of the first piezoelectric cable may be bonded or adhered to at least a portion of a section of negative polarity of the second piezoelectric cable. A method of manufacturing a hydrophone may include winding or coiling a first piezoelectric cable and a second piezoelectric cable at the same time to create a series of wound sections including cables, the wound sections alternating with a series of not wound sections including the cables.
claims:
Claims 1. A hydrophone comprising: a first piezoelectric cable comprising alternating sections of positive polarity and negative polarity; and a second piezoelectric cable comprising alternating sections of negative polarity and positive polarity, where at least a portion of each section of positive polarity of the first piezoelectric cable is bonded to at least a portion of a section of negative polarity of the second piezoelectric cable. 2. The hydrophone of claim 1, comprising an electrically conductive coating covering at least a portion of the first piezoelectric cable and at least a portion of the second piezoelectric cable. 3. The hydrophone of claim 1, comprising coiled sections and uncoiled sections, the coiled sections alternating with the uncoiled sections, each coiled section comprising the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable. 4. The hydrophone of claim 3, wherein in each combination of adjacent coiled section and uncoiled section, one of the first piezoelectric cable or the second piezoelectric cable is cut. 5. The hydrophone of claim 1, wherein the first piezoelectric cable comprises an inner conductor and a layer of piezoelectric copolymer coating and the second piezoelectric cable comprises an inner conductor and a layer of piezoelectric copolymer coating. 6. The hydrophone of claim 1, comprising a series of coiled sections forming a series- connected set of hydrophone sensing units. 7. A method of manufacturing a hydrophone, the method comprising: 

 winding a first piezoelectric cable and a second piezoelectric cable at the same time to create a series of wound sections comprising the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable, the wound sections alternating with a series of not wound sections comprising the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable. 8. The method of claim 7, comprising: polarizing the first piezoelectric cable in a plurality of sections of alternating polarity; and polarizing the second piezoelectric cable in a plurality of sections of alternating polarity. 9. The method of claim 7, comprising bonding adjacent coils in the wound sections. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein the wound sections form tubes. 11. The method of claim 7, comprising cutting one of the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable at a point within each adjacent wound and not wound section. 12. The method of claim 7, comprising winding the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable such that in each wound section the first piezoelectric cable has a polarity opposite the polarity of the second piezoelectric cable. 13. The method of claim 7, comprising coating each wound section with conductive coating. 14. The method of claim 7, comprising winding the first piezoelectric cable and the second piezoelectric cable on a mandrel and comprising periodically removing a wound section from the mandrel to create not wound sections. 15. A hydrophone comprising: a first piezoelectric cable and a second piezoelectric cable arranged together in alternating coiled sections and uncoiled sections. 16. The hydrophone of claim 15, comprising an electrically conductive coating covering at least a portion of the first piezoelectric cable and at least a portion of the second piezoelectric cable. 17. The hydrophone of claim 15, wherein for each coiled section and uncoiled section that are adjacent to each other, one of the first piezoelectric cable or the second piezoelectric cable is cut. 18. The hydrophone of claim 15, wherein the first piezoelectric cable comprises an inner conductor and a layer of piezoelectric copolymer coating and the second piezoelectric cable comprises an inner conductor and a layer of piezoelectric copolymer coating. 19. The hydrophone of claim 15, wherein the of coiled sections form a series-connected set of hydrophone sensing units. 20. The hydrophone of claim 15, wherein the of coiled sections form a parallel-connected set of hydrophone sensing units.
